prompt: A TransitOps Training & Dispatch system with main features including offline trip seat inventory control, configurable operational associations, and assessment-driven staff readiness. The application serves four roles: Administrator (sets up master data and security), Dispatcher (builds trips and manages seat holds), Content Author (creates questions and scoring rules), and Reviewer/Grader (grades subjective work and performs second review). Dispatchers work in a Trips view with a seat-map editor (selectable/non-selectable seats such as blocked ADA rows or crew-only seats) and a booking panel that calculates availability in real time as seats are selected; a “Hold Seat” action creates a pre-reservation with a 10-minute countdown and auto-release to prevent local overselling when multiple browser tabs are open. Administrators maintain Device/Department/Project Association Configuration in a configuration console using a Table with inline edits and a Drawer for details, mapping departments to devices, sample types, and execution queues, with tags, effective date ranges (MM/DD/YYYY), and price attributes (USD) plus validity flags; expired associations are hidden by default but can be toggled visible. For workforce wellness policy, staff can optionally create a Healthy Eating profile (age range, activity level, allergens, goals) and receive personalized daily nutrition budgets and meal suggestions with clear “why this was recommended” explanations (e.g., sodium cap, protein target) and one-click swaps for equivalent portions. Training readiness is managed through full-lifecycle Question Management where authors can create/edit/copy/deactivate, soft-delete and restore items, define question types (single/multiple choice, true/false, fill-in-the-blank, short answer), and set difficulty (1–5), score (0–100), explanation, tags/knowledge points, and applicability scope by department. Built as a Svelte + TypeScript pure frontend SPA with responsive routing and modal-driven workflows, all business logic runs in a browser-side service layer that reads local JSON configuration, computes seat availability, and enforces grading rules. Persistence uses IndexedDB for primary records (trips, seat maps, holds, questions, attempts, grades, association catalogs) and LocalStorage for lightweight preferences (last filter, quiet hours, UI language set to English). The Messaging & Notification Center is strictly in-app only (no SMS/email/IM): it supports template variables (e.g., {tripId}, {score}), user subscription preferences, quiet hours (default 9:00 PM–7:00 AM), rate limiting (max 30 notifications/minute/user), retries (3 attempts) with a dead-letter inbox, and delivery/read timestamps. Scoring supports objective auto-scoring immediately on submit and manual grading with partial credit (0.5-point increments), grader comments, second review required for any score change over 10 points, and configurable rounding (nearest 0.5) and weighting by question type. Pseudo-login uses locally stored usernames with salted password hashes; sensitive fields (health preferences, grader notes) are encrypted at rest in IndexedDB using Web Crypto, and exports/imports are performed via local file download/upload using Blob/FileSaver with JSON schema validation and a SHA-256 fingerprint to detect tampering.
